title: Seismic velocity structure of the Taltal segment of the north Chilean subduction zone from local earthquake tomography
abstract: Local earthquake tomography (LET) is applied to the Taltal segment of the north Chilean subduction zone (~25°–27°S) using a temporary land and offshore seismic network. The derived 3D P-wave velocity model images the subducting Nazca plate, forearc structure, and the seismogenic zone. Velocity anomalies are correlated with seismogenic coupling and fluid distribution in the interplate zone.

location.description: Taltal segment of north Chilean subduction zone ~25°–27°S; 3D LET using temporary seismic network

seismic_lines.name: Temporary seismic network — Taltal segment ~25°–27°S (LET study)

acquisition.vessel: Land and offshore seismic network deployment

data.name: Temporary Seismic Network — Taltal Segment ~25°–27°S (LET passive data)
data.description: Passive seismic data from temporary land and offshore network at Taltal segment (~25°–27°S) used for 3D local earthquake tomography; images subducting Nazca plate, forearc structure and seismogenic zone velocity anomalies

_data_note: G-Cubed 2024 paper; behind paywall. Temporary network deployment; data repository not confirmed.

processing.summary: 3D P-wave velocity model of the Taltal segment (~25°–27°S) derived by local earthquake tomography using a temporary seismic network. Results image the subducting Nazca plate geometry and forearc structure, with velocity anomalies correlated to seismogenic coupling and fluid distribution.
processing.notes: G-Cubed 2024 paper; companion to Bello-González et al. 2022 JSAMES LET study (30°–35°S)

analysis_notes: G-Cubed 2024 paper; behind paywall. Limited information. Temporary seismic network; repository not confirmed.
